tldr: Akamai is a CDN/edge platform; Pkl is Apple's open-source configuration language. You are not replacing the CDN — you are replacing how its configuration is authored and managed by adopting Pkl as the source of truth.
why_switch: Type-checked, validated configuration instead of sprawling JSON or UI clicks.
Reusable modules and templates across environments.
Configuration lives in version control with code review.
Reduces drift between staging, production, and disaster-recovery setups.
what_to_export: All Akamai property configurations (rules, behaviors, criteria)
export_method: Pull current Akamai configurations via the Property Manager API or Akamai CLI as JSON. Export DNS zones from Edge DNS, WAF policies from App & API Protector, and EdgeWorkers code from the EdgeWorkers API. Capture environment-specific values (origin hostnames, TTLs, allowlists) into a structured spreadsheet or YAML so they can be modeled in Pkl. Use the export as the input for your Pkl schema design.
import_method: Pkl does not deploy to Akamai by itself — it is a language for producing configuration. Install Pkl locally, then design Pkl schemas that mirror Akamai's property and behavior structure. Define a base module with shared defaults and per-environment modules that override values. Render Pkl into the JSON/YAML formats that Akamai's API expects, then push the rendered output via the Property Manager API or Terraform Akamai provider. Wire this into CI so every change is type-checked, peer-reviewed, and applied through an automated pipeline. Store secrets outside Pkl files, injected at render time. Validate by diffing rendered output against the existing live configuration before cutting over to Pkl-driven changes.

concept_mapping.target: Pkl per-env modules with shared base

concept_mapping.note: Code stays in its own repo

concept_mapping.target: Pull request + CI render + API push

gotchas: Pkl does not call Akamai APIs; you still need a deploy step (Terraform, custom script, or CLI).
Schema design upfront pays off — rushing leads to brittle modules.
Drift between Pkl source and live Akamai config must be policed in CI.
Secrets must never live inside Pkl files committed to git.
Team needs a brief learning curve on Pkl syntax and tooling.
Plan a freeze window when cutting over from manual edits to Pkl-driven workflow.

email.subject: Heads up: configuration moving to Pkl over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we are moving our edge and CDN configuration into Pkl, an open-source configuration language. The underlying platform does not change — only how we author and review configuration.

What to expect:
- All config changes will go through pull requests and CI.
- Rendered output is diffed before any push to the live platform.
- A short freeze on manual UI edits during cutover.
- Same endpoints, same behavior; no user-visible changes are planned.

If you notice anything unexpected during the cutover window, reply with the URL, timestamp, and details and we will look into it.

Thanks,

Your customer
